moveAnnotations.annotation: This is the Nimzo-Larsen Attack, an offbeat but principled opening that immediately challenges Black's control of the center. We're preparing to fianchetto our bishop on b2, where it will eye the long diagonal (a1-h8) and put pressure on e5. Unlike 1.e4 or 1.d4, this move prioritizes piece placement and flexibility over immediately staking a central claim—we're essentially saying we'll control the center indirectly. This opening is popular with tactically-minded players who want to avoid main-line theory and give Black plenty of rope to hang themselves with in the middlegame.
